suggestion: SCROLL COMPLETION SIGNAL: Find a visible total count on the page
finding: No visible total count found anywhere on the page. No 'Showing X of Y' pattern, no product count in heading, no category counts in sidebar. The sidebar shows aisle names (Food, Home & Kitchen, Baby, etc.) without counts. The heading is just 'Flyer Items' with no number. The only count-related data-testid is 'iceberg-main-nav-cart-button-count' (cart count, not product count).
currentSelector: None — skill uses scroll-until-stable heuristic (count stops increasing after 20 attempts)
proposedChange: No total count signal available. Keep the current scroll-until-stable approach but reduce stableChecks threshold: the current flyer has only 49 items that all load on initial page load without scrolling. Add a fast-exit: if scrolling doesn't increase count after 2 consecutive checks (instead of waiting for 20 max attempts), stop early. This avoids wasting time on small flyers.
notes: This week's flyer only has 49 products total — all loaded on first render with no lazy loading needed. The scroll-until-stable heuristic still works but 20 max attempts is overkill. Consider reducing to 10.

suggestion: DOM WALK-UP VALIDATION: Verify the 5-level parent walk-up is correct
finding: DRIFT DETECTED. The minimum correct container level is 3, not 5. Level 3 (DIV.css-qoklea) is the tightest ancestor that contains all data-testid fields (sale-price, was-price, product-package-size, product-brand, price-descriptor). Levels 4 and 5 also work because they each contain exactly 1 product-title — no cross-contamination. But level 3 is the optimal scope. The DOM path is: product-title -> DIV.css-0 (level 1) -> A.chakra-linkbox__overlay (level 2) -> DIV.css-qoklea (level 3, correct container) -> DIV.chakra-linkbox (level 4) -> DIV.css-yyn1h (level 5, current skill target) -> DIV.css-0 (level 6) -> DIV.css-1tjthuk (level 7) -> LI (level 8).
proposedChange: Change walk-up from 5 to 3 levels. Updated extraction: for(var j=0;j<3;j++){if(c.parentElement)c=c.parentElement;}. Level 3 is the tightest correct scope. Both level 3 and 5 produce identical results today, but level 3 is more resilient — if a wrapper div is removed, level 5 could land on a shared ancestor containing multiple cards.
notes: Tested extraction at both level 3 and level 5 — identical output for all 49 products. Level 3 is safer against future DOM simplification.

suggestion: BATCH SIZE: Check total product count and single-pass extraction
finding: Total products this week: 49. Single-pass extraction produced 7,947 bytes of JSON — well within any reasonable output limit. All 49 products extracted successfully in one call with 0 truncation. Null rates: name 0%, salePrice 59% (29 items are regular-price-only, not on sale), wasPrice 59%, regularPrice 41%, priceDesc 59%, size 0%, brand 41%.
proposedChange: Single-pass extraction works fine for current flyer size (49 items, ~8KB JSON). The skill's suggestion to batch at 50 is reasonable as a safety measure for larger flyers. No change needed, but the drift detection should flag if the flyer exceeds 200 items (which would need batching).
notes: The 59% null rate on salePrice is normal — those 29 items only have regularPrice (not on sale). Content hash: 535818725.

suggestion: BUTTON FALLBACK SELECTORS: Find semantic attributes for Grid View and Complete Flyer buttons
finding: Grid View: bare <span> with no attributes inside <a> with href containing '/online-grocery/flyer'. The <a> sits inside div.view-mode-toggle. No aria-label, no data-testid, no role. Complete Flyer: <a> tag with class='complete-flyer' and href containing '/online-grocery/flyer?...deal%3AFlyer'. Sits inside nav.view-mode-toggle.flyerGridFilters. No aria-label, no data-testid. The Flyer toggle counterpart is an <a> with href='/flyer' and data-core-link=true.
currentSelector: Text match: [...querySelectorAll('button, div, a, span')].find(el => el.textContent.trim() === 'Grid View')
proposedChange: Priority fallback chain for Grid View: (1) div.view-mode-toggle a[href*='online-grocery/flyer'] (structural + href), (2) text match 'Grid View'. For Complete Flyer: (1) a.complete-flyer (class selector), (2) nav.flyerGridFilters a[href*='deal'] (structural + href), (3) text match 'Complete Flyer'. The class selectors are much more reliable than text matching.
notes: The div.view-mode-toggle container is the most stable anchor — it wraps both Grid View and Flyer toggle options. The class 'complete-flyer' on the Complete Flyer tab is a semantic class name, not a utility class, so it's relatively drift-resistant.

suggestion: COMPLETE FLYER VALIDATION: Count products before and after clicking Complete Flyer
finding: Before clicking Complete Flyer (on 'My Suggestions' tab): 51 products loaded, 'Found: 58 Deals' displayed. After clicking Complete Flyer: 52 products initially loaded, 'Found: 1,426 Deals' displayed. URL changed from /flyer?filter=suggestion-for-you to /flyer?filter=deal%3AFlyer+%26+Deals. The page navigates to a new URL rather than just switching a tab.
currentSelector: No validation currently — skill just clicks and proceeds
proposedChange: After clicking Complete Flyer, verify: (1) div.product-filter-container contains text matching /Found:\s*[\d,]+\s*Deals/ (2) the count is > 100 (My Suggestions shows ~58, Complete Flyer shows ~1,426). If count is still < 100 after clicking, the tab click failed. Also parse the 'Found: N Deals' number and use it as the Load More target.
notes: The 'Found: N Deals' text lives in div.product-filter-container. The number includes commas for thousands (e.g., '1,426'). This is a much more reliable completion signal than the 'Found: N Deals' pattern mentioned in the current skill (which references it but doesn't provide the selector).

suggestion: CATEGORY REFERENCE LIST: Extract all visible category names from the filter sidebar
finding: After expanding 'Show 15 More' in the Aisles section, the full aisle list is: Baby (13), Beer & Wine (1), Beverages (128), Bread & Bakery Products (76), Cooked Meals (62), Dairy & Eggs (114), Deli & Prepared Meals (44), Fish & Seafood (23), Frozen (174), Fruits & Vegetables (94), Health & Beauty (8), Household & Cleaning (78), Meat & Poultry (51), Natural & Organic (2), Pantry (360), Pet Care (12), Snacks (293), Value Pack (26), Vegan & Vegetarian Food (18), World Cuisine (25). Deal types: Flyer & Deals (1,426), All Deals (3,400), moi Promotions (214).
currentSelector: No category reference in skill — classification is done by product name heuristic
proposedChange: Add these 20 aisle categories as a reference mapping for the category classification step. Map Food Basics aisles to the skill's category enum: Fruits & Vegetables -> produce, Meat & Poultry -> meat, Fish & Seafood -> seafood, Dairy & Eggs -> dairy, Bread & Bakery Products -> bakery, Frozen -> frozen, Pantry -> pantry, Beverages -> beverages, Snacks -> snacks, Household & Cleaning -> household, Health & Beauty -> personal_care, Baby -> baby, Pet Care -> pet, Deli & Prepared Meals -> deli, Cooked Meals -> deli, World Cuisine -> pantry, Natural & Organic -> pantry, Value Pack -> other, Beer & Wine -> beverages, Vegan & Vegetarian Food -> pantry.
notes: Category counts are per-week and will change. The category NAMES are stable (structural). The 'Show 15 More' button must be clicked to reveal the full aisle list.

suggestion: POSITION-BASED FALLBACK FOR SIZE: Check p.text-sm specificity per card
finding: Exactly 1 p.text-sm element per card across all 6 sampled cards. Every instance matches the size pattern /\d+\s*(g|ml|kg|lb|L)/i. Format is consistent: '165 G ($3.33 per 100g)' or '480 ML ($0.94 per 100ml)'. No false positives found — the selector is currently reliable despite being generic.
proposedChange:
The current selector works but add a validation fallback: after selecting p.text-sm, verify the text matches /\d+\s*(G|ML|KG|LB|L)\b/i. If it doesn't match, try the alternative approach: find the first

inside the price info container (div.flex.flex-col.gap-1) that contains a size pattern. This way if another p.text-sm appears on the card in the future, the regex filter prevents false matches.
notes: 1 p.text-sm per card with 100% size pattern match rate across all tested cards. Risk is low today but the selector is generic — any new small text added to cards could introduce false positives.

suggestion: SCENE BADGE SELECTOR: Check #Scene_Card_exclusive_offers usage and alternatives
finding: 3 elements share the ID #Scene_Card_exclusive_offers (invalid HTML). 2 are SCENE+ OFFER badges with class 'offerstop' — these are the traditional black banner badges at the top of cards. 1 is a points badge showing '75PTS' with completely different classes (no 'offerstop' class, different layout classes). The 'offerstop' class is a semantic class (not Tailwind utility) shared only by the SCENE+ OFFER badges. No data attributes, no aria-labels on any badge.
proposedChange: Use class-based selector instead of ID: (1) div.offerstop for SCENE+ OFFER and Member Price badges (the black banner at card top), (2) For points badges (75PTS etc.), these lack the offerstop class — query for elements with text matching /\d+\s*PTS/ within the card's image area. Fallback: keep #Scene_Card_exclusive_offers as secondary. The offerstop class is semantic and more resilient than the duplicate ID.
notes: The 'offerstop' class matched exactly the 2 SCENE+ OFFER badges. The points badge (75PTS) uses #Scene_Card_exclusive_offers but NOT offerstop — it's a different badge type with different rendering.

suggestion: LOAD MORE EARLY EXIT: Track count deltas across clicks
finding: Perfectly consistent: every Load More click adds exactly 15 products. Tested 10 clicks: 15->30->45->60->75->90->105->120->135->150->165. Delta is always 15 with zero variance across all 10 clicks. No throttling, no variable batch sizes.
proposedChange: The current exit condition (button absent for 3 checks) is already optimal. The consistent +15 per click means a delta-based exit (e.g., 'stop if delta is 0') would be equivalent. However, add a supplementary check: if delta drops to 0 but the button is still present, the page may be throttling — wait 4 seconds instead of 2 before the next attempt. Also: since each click adds exactly 15, you can estimate remaining clicks as ceil((sidebarTotal - loadedCount) / 15) and log progress.
notes: 15 items per click is consistent this week. This may vary by flyer or if FreshCo changes their pagination. The sidebar category total (771 this week) divided by 15 = ~52 clicks needed for full load.

suggestion: TAILWIND DRIFT RESILIENCE: Check for structural/semantic fallbacks on key selectors
finding:
Zero data attributes and zero aria-labels on ANY product card element. The only non-Tailwind classes are: 'card-title' (on the product name

) and 'offerstop' (on Scene+ badges). All price elements are bare tags with only Tailwind utility classes. However, structural position is 100% stable: inside the price row (p.flex-wrap), child[0] is always sale price, child[1] is always regular price (with line-through), child[2] is always the save badge container. This held across all 10 tested cards.
proposedChange:
Add position-based fallback extraction that doesn't depend on color classes: (1) Find price row via p.flex-wrap or the first

with 3 SPAN children inside the card info section (div.p-4). (2) Sale price = child[0] (always first span, contains $ pattern). (3) Regular price = child[1] (always has line-through). (4) Save badge = child[2]. (5) Product title: use p.card-title (the only semantic class) instead of .card-title span.text-grey900. This survives Tailwind color renames while the current selectors break.
notes: Position stability verified across 10 cards with 100% consistency. The p.card-title class is the single most resilient selector — it's a semantic class, not a Tailwind utility. If only one fallback is added, prioritize this one.

finding: Total products: 49. All loaded on first page render (no scroll needed). Null rates: name 0%, brand 41%, salePrice 59% (29 non-sale items), wasPrice 59%, regularPrice 41%, priceDesc 59%, size 0%. Content hash: 535818725. Products span produce, dairy, snacks, beverages, and pantry categories.
notes: 49 products is unusually low. The No Frills flyer page at /en/deals/flyer shows only flyer sale items, not the full store catalog. 20 items (41%) are regular-price produce with no sale — these would be skipped by the skill's 'skip no salePrice' rule, leaving ~20 actual deals.

finding: Found: 1,426 Deals displayed after clicking Complete Flyer. Initial load shows 52 products. Selectors all work: .default-product-tile (52 cards), .head__title, .head__unit-details, .pricing__before-price, .pricing__sale-price, .pricing__secondary-price all return data. 20 aisle categories visible in sidebar.
notes: Only 52 of 1,426 products loaded before Load More clicking. Full extraction would require ~92 Load More clicks at ~15 items per batch. Did not run full load due to time constraints, but selectors verified on loaded subset.

notes: 0% null rate across all fields on 165 extracted products — the current selectors are working perfectly. Full flyer is 771 items requiring ~52 Load More clicks total.
